sources.label: Globe and Mail - Nabtesco finalizes hydraulic split, majority stake sale to Comer Industries (closed Jan 1, 2026; background, pre-Jun 6)
Nabtesco - RVmini / Monocrank compact precision reducer launches (Dec 2025; background)

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since June 6 and a flat tape today (-0.02%); the hydraulic carve-out to Comer Industries closed January 1 and is already in the price. On a discovery-weighted robotics list, a well-owned, +26.9% YTD pure-play reducer name with no fresh evidence stays exactly where it is, consistent with yesterday's hold.
verdicts.bull: Nabtesco remains the dominant RV precision-reducer supplier and a near-unavoidable BOM line for humanoid rotary joints, with capacity expansion underway and the portfolio now cleaner post hydraulic divestiture.
verdicts.neutral: Humanoid exposure is real but the market knows it: +26.9% YTD with no incremental design-win or volume-order news since early June leaves the setup as drift, not signal.
verdicts.bear: Cycloidal RV gears are heavy for humanoid joints versus harmonic and planetary alternatives, so the humanoid narrative could leak to competitors while core industrial-robot demand from China stays soft.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 results on August 5, 2026 and any disclosed humanoid-platform reducer orders or RV capacity-doubling milestones.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowded humanoid-component positioning unwinding if humanoid OEMs standardize on harmonic/planetary actuators rather than RV reducers.
